metadata.name: ario_core
metadata.description: AR.IO Core Program - Token, Vaults, Config, Primary Names

docs: AR.IO Core Program
Handles:
- Token configuration and supply tracking (F1-F3)
- Vault system for time-locked tokens (F4-F9)
- Primary name resolution (F42-F46)
- Protocol configuration

instructions.name: admin_repair_config
docs: Admin recovery — repair `ArioConfig.mint` / `.treasury` when devnet
genesis was partially-initialized (e.g., devnet-setup.ts crashed
between `initializeCore` and `initializeArns` and a re-run created
a new mint). Pre-cutover only — disabled once `finalize_migration`
flips `migration_active` to false.

instructions.name: approve_primary_name
docs: Approve a primary name request (F43)
ADR-016 reshape: caller must be the AntRecord.owner for the requested
name. `ant_program_id` selects which program's AntRecord PDA to look
up — see request_and_set_primary_name for the same authorization
rationale.

accounts.name: primary_name
docs: GAP-2: init_if_needed allows overwriting existing primary name (matches Lua auto-remove)

accounts.name: primary_name_reverse
docs: BUG-1: Reverse lookup PDA for uniqueness enforcement
Seeds: ["primary_name_reverse", hash(request.name.to_lowercase())]

accounts.name: name_owner
docs: Name owner — must be the ANT NFT holder (Metaplex Core asset).
Client must pass ArnsRecord as remaining_accounts[0] and ANT asset as remaining_accounts[1].

instructions.name: close_expired_request
docs: Close an expired primary name request (permissionless pruning)
Matches Lua: primaryNames.prunePrimaryNameRequests

accounts.name: payer
docs: Anyone can close an expired request (permissionless)

instructions.name: create_vault
docs: Create a new time-locked vault (F4)

instructions.name: extend_vault
docs: Extend the lock duration of an existing vault (F7)
Cannot extend an already-expired vault.
Remaining time + extension must not exceed MAX_VAULT_DURATION.

instructions.name: finalize_migration
docs: Permanently disable migration imports (main authority only)

instructions.name: finalize_supply
docs: Set supply totals during migration

instructions.name: import_account
docs: Import a pre-serialized account during migration

instructions.name: import_balance
docs: Typed migration import for `Balance` accounts.
Exists primarily to surface `Balance` in the IDL so off-chain tooling
(snapshot encoder, SDK decoder) can use the Codama-generated codec
instead of hand-rolled discriminator + Borsh bytes.

accounts.name: balance
docs: `init` (not `init_if_needed`) so a re-import for the same owner is
rejected with `account already in use`. Solana txs are atomic, so
there's no partial-success state to recover from — if this PDA
exists, the import already ran and (for pre-registered owners)
already moved SPL tokens. Failing loud beats silent double-transfer
when the orchestrator's progress tracker is wrong.

accounts.name: protocol_token_account
docs: Treasury source for the SPL transfer. Must be `config.treasury`
(the protocol_token_account on the ARIO mint). Owned (SPL-level)
by the ArioConfig PDA — see `release_treasury_authority` for the
one-shot ownership migration on networks where the treasury was
originally created under a different authority.

accounts.name: recipient_token_account
docs: Recipient ATA for the owner. `init_if_needed` so callers don't
have to pre-create — the migration orchestrator can fan out
imports without a per-holder ATA-creation pre-pass.

accounts.name: recipient_owner
docs: `associated_token::authority` constraint above and by the `owner`
instruction-arg constraint here (they must match — the import is
for `owner`'s share, not some third party's).

instructions.name: increase_vault
docs: Add more tokens to an existing vault (F8)

instructions.name: initialize
docs: Initialize the AR.IO protocol configuration
Called once at program deployment

accounts.name: mint
docs: The ARIO token mint

accounts.name: payer
docs: Bind initialize to the program's upgrade authority. Closes the
front-run window between `solana program deploy` and `initialize`
(audit H1 / Theme C). The constraint uses `Some(payer.key())`
equality so a revoked upgrade authority (`None`) cannot satisfy it
— explicit, unlike `unwrap_or_default()` which would coerce a
revoked authority to System Program.

instructions.name: release_vault
docs: Release expired vault back to owner (F9)

instructions.name: remove_primary_name
docs: Remove primary name association (F44)

accounts.name: primary_name_reverse
docs: BUG-1: Close reverse lookup on primary name removal

instructions.name: remove_primary_name_for_base_name
docs: H1: Remove a primary name from the perspective of the base name owner.
Matches Lua: primary_names.removePrimaryNamesForBaseName
The base name owner can revoke any primary name that uses their ArNS domain.
E.g., owner of "arweave" can revoke "alice_arweave" primary name.
ADR-016 reshape: caller must be the AntRecord.owner for the BASE
name's @ undername. `ant_program_id` selects which program manages it.

accounts.name: primary_name_reverse
docs: BUG-1: Close reverse lookup on primary name removal

accounts.name: name_owner
docs: The ANT NFT holder (must hold the Metaplex Core asset for the ArNS name — validated in handler).
Client must pass ArnsRecord as remaining_accounts[0] and ANT asset as remaining_accounts[1].

instructions.name: request_and_set_primary_name
docs: M2: Request AND set a primary name in one tx (auto-approve for ANT
holders of the matching AntRecord).
ADR-016 reshape (per Atticus's review): ario-core is MPL-agnostic.
The caller passes `ant_program_id` so ario-core knows which program's
AntRecord PDA to look at; the PDA's seed derivation pins
(program_id, ant_mint, undername) so a caller lying about
`ant_program_id` fails the seed check. Authorization is "caller is
the AntRecord.owner for this name" rather than "caller is the
MPL Core asset's NFT holder."

accounts.name: primary_name
docs: GAP-2: init_if_needed allows overwriting existing primary name (matches Lua auto-remove)

accounts.name: primary_name_reverse
docs: BUG-1: Reverse lookup PDA for uniqueness enforcement

accounts.name: initiator_token_account
docs: Initiator's token account for fee payment

accounts.name: protocol_token_account
docs: Protocol token account to receive fee

instructions.name: request_and_set_primary_name_from_funding_plan
docs: Phase 3 of FUND_FROM_PLAN.md: request and set a primary name funded
via a multi-source funding plan.
remaining_accounts layout:
[0..validation_account_count): primary-name validation
([0] ArnsRecord, [1] DemandFactor, [2] AntRecord)
[validation_account_count..):  funding-source PDAs
ADR-016 reshape: takes `ant_program_id` instead of reading the asset.

instructions.name: request_primary_name
docs: Request a primary name for your address (F42)
M1: Charges a fee (matches Lua: primary_names.requestPrimaryName charges getTokenCost)
Fee = PRIMARY_NAME_REQUEST_BASE_FEE * demand_factor / DEMAND_FACTOR_SCALE
remaining_accounts[0] = ArnsRecord PDA for base name (validates record exists)
remaining_accounts[1] = DemandFactor account from ario-arns (for fee calculation)

accounts.name: initiator_token_account
docs: M1: Initiator's token account for fee payment

accounts.name: protocol_token_account
docs: M1: Protocol token account to receive fee

instructions.name: request_primary_name_from_funding_plan
docs: Phase 3 of FUND_FROM_PLAN.md: request a primary name funded via a
multi-source funding plan (CPIs into ario-gar's pay_from_funding_plan).
Lua-faithful — `primaryNames.createPrimaryNameRequest` calls
`gar.getFundingPlan` + `gar.applyFundingPlan` with no parallel
single-source path.
remaining_accounts layout:
[0..validation_account_count): primary-name validation
([0] ArnsRecord PDA, [1] DemandFactor PDA)
[validation_account_count..):  funding-source PDAs forwarded to ario-gar

accounts.name: protocol_token_account
docs: Protocol treasury (receives the primary-name fee).

instructions.name: revoke_vault
docs: Revoke a revocable vault, returning funds to controller (F6)
Can only be revoked while vault is still active (not expired)

instructions.name: transfer
docs: Transfer tokens between accounts using SPL Token

instructions.name: update_config
docs: Update protocol configuration (admin only)

instructions.name: vaulted_transfer
docs: Transfer tokens to recipient in a locked vault (F5)
Creates a vault for the recipient, optionally revocable by sender

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Invalid transfer amount - must be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidVaultExtension
errors.msg: Cannot extend vault - new end time must be after current

errors.name: NotVaultController
errors.msg: Not authorized to revoke this vault

errors.name: VaultExpired
errors.msg: Cannot increase vault amount - vault already expired

errors.name: PrimaryNameRequestNotExpired
errors.msg: Primary name request has not expired yet

errors.name: NotNameOwner
errors.msg: Not authorized to approve this request - must be name owner

errors.name: NoPrimaryName
errors.msg: No primary name set for this address

errors.name: MustRemoveExistingPrimaryName
errors.msg: Must remove existing primary name before setting a new one

errors.name: ArnsRecordNotFound
errors.msg: ArNS record not found for the base name

errors.name: NotAntHolder
errors.msg: Caller is not the ANT NFT holder

errors.name: UndernameRecordOwnerRequired
errors.msg: Undername record not found or caller is not the record owner

errors.name: InvalidAccountData
errors.msg: Invalid account data for migration import

errors.name: InvalidPda
errors.msg: PDA derivation does not match target account

types.name: ArioConfig
docs: Global protocol configuration
PDA: ["ario_config"]

fields.name: authority
docs: Admin authority for protocol updates

fields.name: mint
docs: SPL token mint for ARIO token

fields.name: arns_program
docs: ArNS program ID (for cross-program validation)

fields.name: treasury
docs: Treasury token account for fee collection

fields.name: total_supply
docs: Total token supply (fixed at creation)

fields.name: protocol_balance
docs: Protocol treasury balance

fields.name: circulating_supply
docs: Circulating supply (total - locked - staked)

fields.name: locked_supply
docs: Amount locked in vaults

fields.name: min_vault_duration
docs: Minimum vault lock duration (seconds)

fields.name: max_vault_duration
docs: Maximum vault lock duration (seconds)

fields.name: primary_name_request_expiry
docs: Primary name request expiry (seconds)

fields.name: migration_active
docs: Whether migration import is active (permanently disabled by finalize_migration)

fields.name: migration_authority
docs: Dedicated migration authority (hot key for batch imports)

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: Balance
docs: User token balance for protocol accounting
Note: Actual tokens held in SPL token accounts, this tracks protocol state
PDA: ["balance", user_pubkey]

fields.name: owner
docs: Account owner

fields.name: amount
docs: Available balance (not locked)

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: ConfigUpdatedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted on every `update_config` field mutation. One event per
mutated field — if a single tx changes both `min_vault_duration` and
`primary_name_request_expiry`, two events fire.
`field` discriminator matches the `CORE_CONFIG_FIELD_*` constants in
`crate` (re-exported as `CORE_CONFIG_FIELD_MIN_VAULT_DURATION`,
`CORE_CONFIG_FIELD_MAX_VAULT_DURATION`,
`CORE_CONFIG_FIELD_PRIMARY_NAME_REQUEST_EXPIRY`,
`CORE_CONFIG_FIELD_NEW_AUTHORITY`). Values are stable forever — only
append new ones.
`u8` discriminator beats `String { field_name }` here: same indexer
utility, fewer wire bytes.

fields.name: field
docs: See `crate::CORE_CONFIG_FIELD_*` constants.

fields.name: new_value
docs: New value, encoded based on `field`:
- `MIN_VAULT_DURATION` (0): little-endian u64 in bytes 0..8, zero-padded
- `MAX_VAULT_DURATION` (1): little-endian u64, zero-padded
- `PRIMARY_NAME_REQUEST_EXPIRY` (2): little-endian u64, zero-padded
- `NEW_AUTHORITY` (3): full 32-byte Pubkey

types.name: CoreMigrationFinalizedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted exactly once when migration imports are sealed.
Watershed event — every indexer needs to know "migration closed at
slot N." `total_supply` is captured at the moment of finalization
(post `finalize_supply`), and `slot` is the Clock slot at the
finalize tx.

types.name: FundingSourceKind
docs: Tagged enum describing one source in a funding plan.
Multi-gateway: each source carries its own gateway PDA in remaining_accounts
(no single shared `gateway` slot). Each source consumes a known number of
`remaining_accounts` slots in declaration order — the handler iterates and
slices. See `docs/MULTI_GATEWAY_FUNDING_PLAN.md` for the full layout.

types.name: FundingSourceSpec
docs: One entry in the funding plan vector.

types.name: PrimaryName
docs: Active primary name assignment
PDA: ["primary_name", owner_pubkey]

fields.name: owner
docs: Address this primary name resolves to

fields.name: name
docs: The primary name

fields.name: set_at
docs: When the primary name was set

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: PrimaryNameRemovedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted when a primary name is removed.
`caller != owner` is the base-name override path
(`remove_primary_name_for_base_name`); `caller == owner` is the
holder's own removal (`remove_primary_name`). One event covers both
to keep the wire surface compact — consumers branch on the caller
equality rather than subscribing to two event types.

types.name: PrimaryNameRequest
docs: Primary name request (pending approval)
PDA: ["primary_name_request", initiator_pubkey]

fields.name: initiator
docs: Address requesting primary name

fields.name: name
docs: Name being requested (must be owned by someone)

fields.name: created_at
docs: Request creation timestamp

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Request expiry timestamp

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: PrimaryNameRequestExpiredEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted when an expired primary-name request is reaped.
`refunded` is the lamports returned to the initiator (rent recovered
when the PDA is closed). The request fee is paid up front and is NOT
refunded — only the rent reservation comes back. Indexers consume
this to drop pending-request UI rows once they expire.

types.name: PrimaryNameRequestedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted when a primary name *request* is created.
Distinct from `PrimaryNameSetEvent` — set fires from the
auto-approve path (`request_and_set_primary_name*`) and from
`approve_primary_name`, while this event covers the request-only
path that needs explicit approval (or expiry).
`funding_source` matches the wire encoding used by ario-arns
(`FUNDING_SOURCE_*` constants in ario-arns and re-exported here as
`crate::FUNDING_SOURCE_BALANCE` / `crate::FUNDING_SOURCE_FUNDING_PLAN`):
`0 = Balance` (single-source SPL transfer from the initiator's ATA),
`4 = FundingPlan` (multi-source CPI through ario-gar
`pay_from_funding_plan`).

fields.name: funding_source
docs: See `crate::FUNDING_SOURCE_*` (matches ario-arns wire encoding).

types.name: PrimaryNameReverse
docs: Reverse lookup: name → owner. Ensures a name can only be set as primary for one address.
PDA: ["primary_name_reverse", hash(name.to_lowercase())]

fields.name: name
docs: The primary name (lowercase)

fields.name: owner
docs: The owner who has this as their primary name

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: PrimaryNameSetEvent
docs: Emitted when primary name is set

types.name: SupplyFinalizedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted exactly once when supply totals are sealed via
`finalize_supply`.
Genesis-equivalent: pins the canonical `total_supply` and `decimals`
for the ARIO mint. Consumed by indexers to seed their supply-tracking
dashboards from chain state alone.

types.name: TransferEvent
docs: Emitted when tokens are transferred

types.name: Vault
docs: Locked token vault
PDA: ["vault", owner_pubkey, vault_id (as le_bytes)]

fields.name: owner
docs: Vault owner (receives tokens when unlocked)

fields.name: vault_id
docs: Unique vault ID for this owner

fields.name: amount
docs: Locked token amount

fields.name: start_timestamp
docs: Lock start timestamp

fields.name: end_timestamp
docs: Unlock timestamp

fields.name: controller
docs: Optional controller for revocable vaults

fields.name: revocable
docs: Whether this vault is revocable by controller

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: VaultCounter
docs: Tracks next vault ID per user
PDA: ["vault_counter", owner_pubkey]

fields.name: owner
docs: Vault owner

fields.name: next_id
docs: Next vault ID to use

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: VaultCreatedEvent
docs: Emitted when a vault is created

types.name: VaultExtendedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted when an existing vault's lock duration is extended.
`new_end_timestamp` is the post-extension expiry, so a consumer
indexing this stream can update vault state without reading the
account back.

types.name: VaultIncreasedEvent
docs: PR-5: emitted when additional tokens are added to an existing vault.
`new_balance` mirrors `Vault.amount` post-increase so consumers don't
have to fetch the account.

types.name: VaultReleasedEvent
docs: Emitted when a vault is released

types.name: VaultRevokedEvent
docs: Emitted when a vault is revoked
